items.scenario: You are a student in an advanced economics seminar. You were assigned a research presentation for next Tuesday, but you just learned the library will close its archive room that day. You need the professor's help to adjust your plan.
items.direction: Write an email to your professor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the problem you have run into
Ask whether your presentation can be moved to a later date
Suggest an alternative source you could use instead
items.subject: Help with my seminar presentation schedule

items.scenario: You recently received your midterm grade for a chemistry lab report. The grade seems lower than you expected based on the feedback comments, and you would like to understand the scoring before the next assignment.
items.direction: Write an email to your teaching assistant. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe which part of the grade you find confusing
Ask how the points were calculated for the report
Mention what you plan to improve next time
items.subject: Question about my lab report grade

items.scenario: You signed up for a weekend photography workshop run by the campus arts center. The confirmation email listed a different start time than the one posted on the website, and you want to make sure you arrive correctly.
items.direction: Write an email to the workshop coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the conflicting times you received
Ask which start time is correct
Mention that you may need to arrange transportation
items.subject: Confirming the photography workshop start time

items.scenario: You are the treasurer of the student debate club. The club booked a meeting hall for its annual tournament, but the venue just told you the projector listed in the package is unavailable for that date.
items.direction: Write an email to the venue manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why the projector matters for the event
Ask whether a replacement can be provided
Suggest a backup option if none is available
items.subject: Projector issue for our tournament booking

items.scenario: You finished the first week of a summer marketing internship. Your supervisor mentioned a training session on the company database, but you never received the login details you were promised.
items.direction: Write an email to your supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank your supervisor for the first-week guidance
Ask when the database login details will arrive
Explain why you need them before the training
items.subject: Following up on database access for training

items.scenario: You live in a campus dormitory. The laundry room on your floor has had a broken dryer for over a week, and residents are now waiting a long time to use the single working machine.
items.direction: Write an email to the residence hall manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the laundry room
Ask when the broken dryer will be fixed
Suggest a temporary arrangement for residents
items.subject: Broken dryer in the dormitory laundry room

items.scenario: Your friend invited you to join a hiking trip this weekend, but you have a part-time shift that afternoon. You would like to join part of the trip and work out the timing together.
items.direction: Write an email to your friend. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank your friend for the invitation
Explain the timing conflict you have
Suggest a way you could still take part
items.subject: About the weekend hiking trip

items.scenario: You joined the campus film club this semester. You attended its first screening night and enjoyed it, but the seating was crowded and many members could not find a place to sit.
items.direction: Write an email to the club organizer. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank the organizer for the screening night
Describe the seating problem you noticed
Suggest a change for the next event
items.subject: Feedback on the first film club screening
